figures.caption: Fig. 1. The pipeline of our proposed LLM-assisted TSC framework, where we employ LLMs to generate rich semantic features based on customized traffic prompts to enhance the downstream MARL decision-making process.
TABLE V OPTIMIZATION PERFORMANCE OF VARIOUS LATS VARIANTS AND ATTENDLIGHT VARIANTS IN THE ABLATION STUDY.
TABLE VI ABLATION STUDY OF DIFFERENT LATS PROMPT VARIANTS ON THE HOMOGENEOUS Grid 5×5 NETWORK AND THE HETEROGENEOUS Monaco NETWORK.

TABLE I COMPARATIVE RESULTS OF DIFFERENT METHODS ON BOTH GRID 5×5 AND MONACO NETWORKS ACROSS AVERAGE EPISODIC METRICS: QUEUE LENGTH (↓), SPEED (↑), INTERSECTION DELAY (↓), COMPLETION RATE (↑), TRIP TIME (↓), AND TRIP DELAY (↓). HERE, ↑ INDICATES HIGHER VALUES ARE BETTER, AND ↓ INDICATES LOWER VALUES ARE BETTER, WITH THE BEST RESULT FOR EACH METRIC IN BOLD AND THE SECOND BEST UNDERLINED.

Fig. 5. Illustration of the three selected intersections (agent 2, agent 9, and agent 23) with diverse topology structures from the heterogeneous Monaco traffic network used for t-SNE visualizations (left). 2D t-SNE visualization of phase features for three selected intersections, comparing LATS and IPPO (LATS w/o TS) during training: (a) LATS after 800 episodes, (b) LATS after 1600 episodes, (c) IPPO after 800 episodes, and (d) IPPO after 1600 episodes (right).
